summary: Flow polling uses fixed delay and dispatcher, making tests slow and timing-dependent.

findings.evidence: stream hard-codes delay(1000) and Dispatchers.IO, so tests cannot control scheduling deterministically.
findings.minimal_fix: Inject dispatcher and polling interval (or scheduler abstraction) and use coroutine test dispatcher in unit tests.
findings.verification: Run tests with StandardTestDispatcher and advanceUntilIdle to verify emissions without real time waits.

findings.evidence: Without scheduler control, validating stream behavior requires slow integration-style timing checks.
findings.minimal_fix: Split emission logic into fast unit-tested loop policy and minimal integration test for real dispatcher wiring.
findings.verification: Run the test suite, measure runtime, and verify stream behavior is covered by fast deterministic tests.

findings.evidence: The service owns API construction and execution policy in one path, limiting isolated testing seams.
findings.minimal_fix: Inject PriceApi and execution policy dependencies explicitly to isolate flow behavior from transport.
findings.verification: Add unit tests with fake PriceApi and verify emitted sequence under controlled scheduler.
